title: Monday.com is cutting 20 percent of its workforce as it pivots to an AI work platform
excerpt: Monday.com is cutting roughly 20 percent of its global workforce, about 620 people, as the Israeli project management company restructures around what it calls its AI Work Platform. The company disclosed the plan in a Form 6-K filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday, citing the need to support a leaner operating […]
content_html:
TL;DR

Monday.com plans to cut about 620 jobs, 20 percent of staff, as it restructures around its AI-driven growth strategy and flatter organisation

Monday.com is cutting roughly 20 percent of its global workforce, about 620 people, as the Israeli project management company restructures around what it calls its AI Work Platform. The company disclosed the plan in a Form 6-K filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday, citing the need to support a leaner operating model aligned with its AI-driven growth strategy. Co-founder and co-CEO Eran Zinman published a note to staff on LinkedIn calling it the most painful decision the company has made since its founding.

The layoffs follow a brutal stretch for Monday.com’s stock, which has lost more than half its value in 2026 and roughly 75 percent from its 52-week high. The company is caught in the broader SaaSpocalypse selloff that has punished enterprise software stocks as investors worry that AI agents and vibe coding could make conventional SaaS tools obsolete. Monday.com’s market capitalisation has fallen to roughly $3 billion, a fraction of the valuations it commanded during the pandemic-era software boom.

Zinman framed the restructuring as an offensive move rather than a defensive one, writing that the company is not making the change to protect what it has but to go all in on what it can become. He said Monday.com had shifted its core vision over the past nine months from managing work to doing the work for customers, with people and AI agents working together in one workspace. The organisation built for the previous chapter, he argued, does not fit the new AI era.

The company expects to incur between $45 million and $55 million in restructuring charges, split between $30 million to $35 million in severance and employee benefits and roughly the same amount in office space impairments, partially offset by about $15 million in non-cash credits for share-based compensation. Most charges are expected to land in the second half of 2026. Monday.com said it plans to continue hiring in key strategic areas even as it cuts elsewhere.

The restructuring mirrors moves by other SaaS companies caught between strong revenue growth and investor anxiety about AI disruption. Fellow Israeli software company Wix cut 20 percent of its staff in May, citing similar pressures from AI competition and a strengthening shekel, while Atlassian cut 1,600 jobs in March and replaced its CTO as part of its own AI pivot. The pattern across the industry is consistent: record or near-record revenues, significant headcount reductions, and savings redirected toward AI.

Zinman addressed the obvious question directly in a FAQ appended to his note, writing that the decision was not made to reduce costs or replace people with AI. He described three structural changes: a flatter organisation with fewer management layers, more autonomous teams with broader ownership, and a new go-to-market model that puts staff closer to customers as they adopt AI. The company said it intends to reinvest the vast majority of savings in people, products, and future growth.

Whether the market believes that framing remains to be seen. Monday.com reported first-quarter revenue of $351 million, up 24 percent year on year, the kind of growth rate that would have delighted investors two years ago. But in a market where AI is simultaneously the justification for cutting staff and the product companies are pivoting toward, the question is whether restructuring announcements like this one represent genuine strategic shifts or what OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has called AI washing, the practice of citing artificial intelligence to justify decisions driven by other pressures entirely.

Published July 22, 2026 - 6:22 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.zh: 市场是否相信这一框架还有待观察。

sentences.en: Monday.com reported first-quarter revenue of $351 million, up 24 percent year on year, the kind of growth rate that would have delighted investors two years ago.
sentences.zh: Monday.com公布第一季度营收为3.51亿美元，同比增长24%，这样的增长率在两年前会让投资者感到高兴。

sentences.en: But in a market where AI is simultaneously the justification for cutting staff and the product companies are pivoting toward, the question is whether restructuring announcements like this one represent genuine strategic shifts or what OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has called AI washing, the practice of citing artificial intelligence to justify decisions driven by other pressures entirely.
sentences.zh: 但在人工智能同时成为裁员和产品公司转向的理由的市场中，问题在于，像这样的重组公告是否代表真正的战略转变，或者OpenAI首席执行官山姆·奥尔特曼(Sam Altman)所说的人工智能清洗，即引用人工智能来证明完全由其他压力驱动的决策合理的做法。

sentences.en: Published July 22, 2026 - 6:22 pm UTC
sentences.zh: 发布时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日 - 下午 6:22（世界标准时间）

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

Monday.com 计划裁员约 620 人，即员工总数的 20%，以围绕人工智能驱动的增长战略和扁平化组织进行重组

以色列项目管理公司 Monday.com 正在围绕其所谓的 AI 工作平台进行重组，因此将裁员约 20%，即约 620 人。 该公司在周二向美国证券交易委员会提交的 6-K 表格中披露了该计划，称需要支持与其人工智能驱动的增长战略相一致的更精简的运营模式。 联合创始人兼联合首席执行官 Eran Zinman 在 LinkedIn 上向员工发布了一份说明，称这是公司自成立以来做出的最痛苦的决定。

此次裁员是在 Monday.com 的股价经历了残酷的扩张之后发生的，该公司的股价在 2026 年已经损失了一半以上，较 52 周高点下跌了约 75%。 该公司陷入了更广泛的 SaaS 末日抛售之中，这对企业软件股票造成了打击，因为投资者担心人工智能代理和氛围编码可能会让传统的 SaaS 工具变得过时。 Monday.com 的市值已降至约 30 亿美元，仅是其在大流行时期软件繁荣时期估值的一小部分。

津曼将此次重组视为一种进攻性举措，而不是一种防御性举措，他写道，公司进行变革并不是为了保护现有的东西，而是为了全力以赴地发展现有的东西。 他表示，Monday.com 在过去九个月中已将其核心愿景从管理工作转变为为客户工作，让人员和人工智能代理在一个工作空间中协同工作。 他认为，为前一章建立的组织不适合新的人工智能时代。

该公司预计将产生 4,500 万至 5,500 万美元的重组费用，其中包括 3,000 万至 3,500 万美元的遣散费和员工福利，以及大致相同金额的办公空间减值，部分被约 1,500 万美元的股票薪酬非现金信贷所抵消。 预计大部分费用将于 2026 年下半年生效。 Monday.com 表示，尽管在其他地方进行了裁员，但计划继续在关键战略领域进行招聘。

此次重组反映了其他 SaaS 公司的举措，这些公司夹在强劲的收入增长和投资者对人工智能颠覆的焦虑之间。 以色列软件公司 Wix 在 5 月份裁减了 20% 的员工，理由是人工智能竞争和谢克尔走强带来了类似的压力，而 Atlassian 在 3 月份裁掉了 1,600 名员工，并更换了首席技术官，作为其自身人工智能转型的一部分。 整个行业的模式是一致的：创纪录或接近创纪录的收入、大幅裁员以及将节省的资金转向人工智能。

津曼在他的笔记所附的常见问题解答中直接回答了这个明显的问题，并写道，做出这一决定并不是为了降低成本或用人工智能取代人类。 他描述了三个结构性变化：管理层更少的扁平化组织、拥有更广泛所有权的更自主的团队，以及新的市场进入模式，使员工在采用人工智能时更接近客户。 该公司表示，打算将绝大多数储蓄再投资于人员、产品和未来增长。

市场是否相信这一框架还有待观察。 Monday.com公布第一季度营收为3.51亿美元，同比增长24%，这样的增长率在两年前会让投资者感到高兴。 但在人工智能同时成为裁员和产品公司转向的理由的市场中，问题在于，像这样的重组公告是否代表真正的战略转变，或者OpenAI首席执行官山姆·奥尔特曼(Sam Altman)所说的人工智能清洗，即引用人工智能来证明完全由其他压力驱动的决策合理的做法。

发布时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日 - 下午 6:22（世界标准时间）

Back to top
